text: Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.
I’m checking the workspace structure and the installed skill instructions first, then I’ll map the existing `pages` router surface so I can migrate it cleanly to the App Router.

output.output:
---
name: mempal-memory
description: Search and record durable engineering knowledge with the mempal CLI, before and after coding work.
---

# Mempal memory protocol for this eval

This environment has a persistent memory database that survives across tasks, reachable through
the `mempal` CLI (already installed, on PATH). Run it with your normal shell tool — there is no
MCP server, and you need exactly two commands.

## 1. Search before you start

```bash
mempal search "<key terms of the task>" --json --top-k 5
```

Run it once, at the start, with the task's key terms (framework, API, error message, symptom).
Empty results are a normal outcome — continue with the task and do not investigate the memory
database. Treat any hit as evidence, not authority: verify it against the current repository
before acting on it.

## 2. Record before you finish

If the work produced a durable engineering decision or a reusable debugging lesson, write a short
markdown note and ingest it:

```bash
cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/<short-slug>.md" <<'EOF'
# <one-line title>

<what was decided or learned, and why — 2-5 sentences, enough for a future task to act on it>
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals
```

Only `mempal search` and `mempal ingest` are needed. Do not call other mempal subcommands
(`status`, `projects`, `brief`, `phase3`, `knowledge*`, `cowork-*`): they print format
specifications and protocol dumps that cost context and tell you nothing about this task.

## What not to store

Never store benchmark answers, accepted proposal numbers, hidden-test guesses, raw transcripts,
or task-specific output that would reveal the answer if the same task is run again. Record the
reusable *why*, not the answer. If nothing reusable was decided, do not invent a note.

    "content": "# Bind Spring Boot port from SERVER_PORT via application properties\n\nFor small HTTP service translations, Spring Boot can mirror a source app's environment-driven port contract without custom bootstrap code by setting `server.port=${SERVER_PORT:3000}` in `src/main/resources/application.properties`.     "content": "# Use updateTag in Next Server Actions for no-stale read-your-writes\n\nIn Next 16, when a Server Action mutates data that is read through a tag-cached path, `updateTag(tag)` is the right invalidation API when the user must not see stale content on the next load. `revalidateTag` can preserve stale-while-revalidate behavior, but `updateTag` expires the tag immediately so the next request blocks on fresh data.",

text:
Plan:
1. Convert shared app shell from `_app` and `_document` into a typed `app/layout.tsx` plus a client provider wrapper.
2. Migrate each page to `app/` using App Router data patterns: server components, `headers()`, `generateMetadata`, `generateStaticParams`, `notFound()`, and client components only where navigation handlers are needed.
3. Replace `pages/api` handlers with typed `app/api/**/route.ts` handlers using `NextRequest`/`NextResponse`.
4. Add App Router error surfaces for the old `404` and `_error` behavior, then delete `pages/` and run a type/build check.

import { type ReadonlyHeaders } from '../web/spec-extension/adapters/headers';
/**
 * This function allows you to read the HTTP incoming request headers in
 * [Server Components](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/rendering/server-components),
 * [Server Actions](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/data-fetching/server-actions-and-mutations),
 * [Route Handlers](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/routing/route-handlers) and
 * [Middleware](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/routing/middleware).
 *
 * Read more: [Next.js Docs: `headers`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/headers)
 */
export declare function headers(): Promise<ReadonlyHeaders>;

input.command:
/bin/bash -lc 'cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/next-app-router-migration-next16.md" <<'"'EOF'
# Next 16 App Router migration details

When migrating a Next.js 16 app from the Pages Router, replace page-level data hooks with App Router primitives: server components, "'`generateStaticParams`, `generateMetadata`, route handlers, and segment error files. In this version, request helpers like `headers()` are async and dynamic route `params` are passed as promises in app pages and route handlers, so typing them that way avoids build-time mismatches.
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals'

text:
Migrated the app fully to the App Router and removed `pages/` entirely.

The shared shell from `_app` and `_document` now lives in [app/layout.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx), with typed client context in [components/AppProvider.tsx](/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx). The old page routes were moved to [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx), [app/blog/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/page.tsx), and [app/blog/[id]/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/[id]/page.tsx), using App Router patterns instead of `getServerSideProps`/`getStaticProps`/`getStaticPaths`. I also replaced `next/head` with `metadata`/`generateMetadata`, replaced `next/router` usage with `next/navigation` or `Link`, and added typed async `params` / `headers()` handling for Next 16.

The old API routes were migrated to typed route handlers at [app/api/posts/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/route.ts) and [app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts), and the old `404` / `_error` behavior is now covered by [app/not-found.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/not-found.tsx) and [app/error.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/error.tsx). Shared data types/fetch helpers are in [lib/blog.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/blog.ts), and [styles/globals.css](/home/user/workspace/styles/globals.css) was updated for the new layout/link patterns.

Verification: `npm run build` completed successfully. Next also updated [tsconfig.json](/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json) to include `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts`. There is no `.git` directory in this workspace, so I couldn’t provide a git status/diff summary.
